chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The one for whom there is no wergild

text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ اطَّلَعَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مِنَ الْجَرِيدِ فَقَالَ لَهُ النَّبِيُّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَوْ أَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ تَثْبُتُ لِي لَقُمْتُ إِلَيْكَ بِالْمِشْقَصِ حَتَّى أَفْقَأَ بِهِ عَيْنَكَ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ أَ ذَاكَ لَنَا فَقَالَ وَيْحَكَ أَوْ وَيْلَكَ أَقُولُ لَكَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَعَلَ تَقُولُ ذَلِكَ لَنَا .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Ubeyd Bin Zurara who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘A man peeked at the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> from the reeds, so the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> said to him: ‘If I<sup>saww</sup> knew you would be standing still for me<sup>saww</sup>, I<sup>saww</sup> would have come to you with the arrow until I<sup>saww</sup> poke your eyes with it’. So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Is that for us (as well)?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Woe be unto you!’, or ‘Suffering be upon you!’ I<sup>asws</sup> am saying to you that Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> did it. You are saying, ‘Is that for us?’
